Overview

About This Book

The NCEES Fundamentals of Surveying (FS) exam and the Principles and Practice of Surveying (PS) exam are the two milestones on the path to becoming a Licensed Professional Surveyor. Maxwell Pepper's FS and PS Exam Prep: A Surveyor's Tale follows a young apprentice and their mentor Prospero through the world of surveying — from GIS and photogrammetry to legal boundaries and professional ethics — with quizzes at the end of every chapter and full-length practice exams for both the FS and PS.

What This Guide Covers

  1. Surveying Mathematics
  2. Field Data Acquisition
  3. Horizontal and Vertical Control
  4. Legal Descriptions & Boundary Surveying
  5. Photogrammetry & GIS
  6. Construction Surveying
  7. Professional Practice & Ethics
Get It on Amazon ↗
Topics

What's Covered

Surveying Mathematics

Traverse calculations, area computation, coordinate geometry (COGO), and least squares adjustment.

Field Data Acquisition

GPS/GNSS methods, total station operation, leveling procedures, and field data collection techniques.

Horizontal and Vertical Control

Control networks, geodetic datums, vertical datums, and how control surveying integrates with modern positioning systems.

Legal Descriptions & Boundary Surveying

Metes and bounds, rectangular system, retracement surveys, adverse possession, and boundary law principles.

Photogrammetry & GIS

Aerial photography, photogrammetric mapping, remote sensing basics, and GIS data integration.

Construction Surveying

Route surveys, earthwork calculations, staking procedures, and as-built surveys.

Professional Practice & Ethics

Licensing requirements, Standards of Practice, ethical obligations, and professional responsibilities.

Is This For You?

Who This Book Helps

  • ✓Surveying graduates preparing to take the NCEES FS exam
  • ✓Licensed Surveyors-in-Training (LSIT) advancing toward the PS exam
  • ✓Candidates who find traditional surveying references dry and difficult to absorb
  • ✓Working surveyors who need a structured review for licensure

Why This Book Is Different

Surveying exam prep materials tend to be tables, formulas, and problem sets with no connecting thread. A Surveyor's Tale gives you a mentor-apprentice framework where every concept is introduced through a real project challenge. The narrative makes abstract topics like least squares adjustment and geodetic datums tangible and memorable.

Ready to Get Started?

Available now on Amazon in paperback and Kindle format.

FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the difference between the FS and PS exams?
The FS (Fundamentals of Surveying) is the entry-level exam for surveying licensure — equivalent to the FE for engineers. The PS (Principles and Practice of Surveying) is the second exam, taken after gaining qualifying work experience. This guide covers both.
How many questions are on the FS exam?
The NCEES FS exam contains 110 questions in two 2-hour sessions. The PS exam format varies — check the current NCEES exam specifications for your exam cycle.
Is the FS exam open book?
Yes. Like the FE exam, the FS exam provides an NCEES-supplied reference during the exam. Knowing how to navigate it efficiently is a key part of exam success.
How long should I study for the FS exam?
Most candidates need 8-12 weeks of structured study. The guide covers all major topic areas with chapter quizzes to track your progress.
Does this guide cover the 2024-2025 NCEES specifications?
Yes. The guide is aligned with the current NCEES FS and PS exam specifications as of 2024-2025.
